itemListElement.reviewBody:
I have written my 2⭐ review previously on 14/12/2025 in this forum regarding AIS, while being "anonymous" and choose to remain so for obvious reasons. I don't prefer sobriquets. I should add some more points regarding Adamas International School (AIS) & similar ICSE schools, which should be kept mind by each and every parent/student reading this post. 

Pardon me for "ANY grammatical mistakes" in this post and my previous post, it's due to the phone's keyboard which auto-corrects some words but which doesn't actually fit in that particular context:

1) ICSE schools often charge exorbitant fees in the name of "holistic" development of a child by providing them extra-curricular activities but to be honest, they aren't so much effective as they are limited to only 1 or 2 periods in an entire week, no matter if it is music/dance/yoga/art classes/swimming or anything else. 1 or 2 instructors simply can't teach a crowd of 30-35 students effectively by interacting with them for 1-2 hours in an entire week. There isn't even any proper interaction on 1:1 basis b/w the teacher and the student for every student in a class. If you are a student or a parent who wants his/her child to excel in a particular extra-curricular activity, arrange for separate classes, a school isn't necessarily the best place for that but there are exceptions I have seen while studying in AIS, and yes I am talking about those students who stayed back after school to learn that skill from the same teachers where the interaction ratio is far better, maybe 3-5 students per teacher. If that's the case, then it's not worth paying for those classes if one is not interested in them and should have the option to opt out of such activities, but the school keeps on collecting money from all the students irrespective of whether they are learning it or not. The option for a "separate payment" should be there for those students who want to learn the skill, but those who don't wanna learn, they should have the facility to "not" pay for that. 

2) Students of ICSE schools are relatively better at english than their contemporaries in CBSE/State board schools: It's the biggest myth. No doubt the level of ICSE english is better than other boards as mentioned above, but if a student or your child's aim is to solely improve English for the sake of competitive exams like UPSC/BANK/CAT exam or to be a fluent speaker in English then you are in the wrong place. English can be improved on your own based on proper & widely available resources online/offline and good habits of reading newspapers/popular novels & learning proper grammar, which is totally dependent on that individual and the school can only provide an environment suitable for that at best, rest it's 100% the individual's effort and it's NOT something magical that getting admitted to any ICSE school will make your child the "Shakespeare" of modern english. Nope! People routinely crack exams like UPSC/CAT/BANK PO which are "english-heavy" despite coming from CBSE/Hindi-medium/Bengali-medium/miscellaneous backgrounds. The real difference is made by that individual who makes it a regular habit to read English newspapers/Novels/etc. and for the spoken part, one can listen to documentaries and English news channels available on YouTube & other platforms. There are plenty of students who fail to crack the toughest exams despite having an ICSE background and there are students who on their own effort, did wonders in these exams and are doing very well in life.

3) The internet is your friend in today's era. Knowledge is no longer limited to schools/colleges. It's just a place to get sufficient grades for passing and getting approx 85-90% marks in Board exams. That's more than sufficient except for specific B.Sc./B.Com./B.A. courses which require a certain percentage in XII board exams > 96%. JEE/NEET gives ample relaxation in this regard. And these board marks don't even matter and are just a qualifier incase of exams like UPSC/BANKING. Yes, in case of the CAT exam, board results do matter but not the marks of each and every standard from I to XII. 

4) ICSE isn't even a pre-requisite for most career oriented college courses! You are simply wasting money in false hopes! It's just a carefully tailored market to lure naive & ill-informed people by promising them world-class infrastructure & similar features, but these are simply gimmicks and CBSE board schools are more than enough. If you are ready to learn by yourself and commit daily, you can be what you want. And to all those parents still in doubt, take the pain to teach your children by learning online. That's the best thing in 2025. Send them to any CBSE/proper state-board affiliated schools. This model is widely followed in Kota where students get admitted to "dummy" schools where they give written/lab exams for boards and rest of the time they are focused in cracking competitive exams which is the real deal.

Conclusion: AIS is overhyped and simply a shameless institution fooling everyone. They know the art of luring the unsuspecting victims (naive parents mostly) who think they are doing justice to the careers of their children. NOT AT ALL! I have been a student of AIS always and I can vouch for whatever I have said. Save you children from such shady institutions.

I would suggest any parent to save their hard-earned money instead because your child can't crack a single competitive exam based on the school's curriculum and teaching expertise. It's a battle which your child will be fighting alone, and it's far better for your child to take admission in any CBSE school because in exams like JEE, GATE, UPSC, CA etc, the school's contribution is next to zero. Don't make this mistake. I studied in this school throughout my life but nothing helped when I faced these exams. Consider school as a place to merely get a certificate for class X,XII, nothing else.

Rather focus on preparing for competitive exams and that will help your child in the long run. The school curriculum is at best mediocre and can't cater to the standards set my the competitive exams in today's world. Mark my words. It's better to train your child yourself and take guidance from the internet in 2025 rather than depend on school. They teach nothing worth paying lakhs of Rs. Better content is available for free online.
Undoubtedly the best school in West Bengal.

My son has gone to swimming competition in this school
This is a very nice school
The management of this school is very good 👍
Best ICSE, ISC, IGCSE School in Kolkata, Nursery to class 11 Admissions Open
Worst school... High fee . No proper care of students

Absolutely worst school with worst environment. The environment is very bad,school management and teachers are totally unhelpful and ignorant. They only pay attention to some "special" students and their parents. Also, the fees is unreasonable and there is no discipline too. Stay away from this school.
We did not like the attitude of the teachers towards the children or the teaching philosophy so we changed school.

Every other school charging lower fees has air conditioning in both its classroom and its bus, so why does this one still not have AC in the classroom even after charging exorbitant fees? My daughter developed an allergy due to the intense heat. Instead of giving their children the very minimum of comfort, like at other schools, they are more concerned about money, which is why they are not even bothered here.

It's very very nice too good they give good food, and they're many activities to do there like swimming Karate yoga library music dance instrument and PT where we can play basketball cricket and football. They give us all the activities to do unlike other schools. But the fees is too much but it worth it and they also need money to do so many things but actually they are not needed but it's compulsory to do and they take the money like this but it's too good the best school I ever seen
